content: Later evidence shows comms loss for .156 / OP station 3 with keep-alive failure and connection reset, concurrent tipper active alarms including RIO ethernet failure and motor/disabled/faulted states, plus heartbeat statistics showing recent heartbeat activity. Separate OPC UA status view shows Hospital1 connected for 8 hours and GLD1 connected for 20 hours at the time of capture, while Tipper7 and Tipper8 were disabled.

title: Candidate cause analysis: network degradation and fragile OPC subscription rebuild behavior
content: Investigation discussion proposed network degradation based on DB event log timeouts, with assumed OPC communications impact. Supporting evidence includes reconnect attempts, monitored item rebuild of 622 items, and multiple WriteOpcTagAsync service result exceptions against Hospital1 tags, suggesting subscription/session recovery behavior after communications loss.

title: Next-day monitoring: single .157 hospital controller disconnect and reconnect
content: On 4/9, support reported only one disconnect that morning. Supporting log evidence shows a 06:37:37 connection reset on 10.27.80.157:4840 with subsequent subscription transfer failures and resend success. Discussion clarified that .157 is the hospital controller and that the focus was station disconnects rather than AGV disconnects.

title: Coordinated recovery actions involved master e-stop, OptiSweep restart, and tipper pause/unpause
content: Recovery was performed through coordinated actions between support and site personnel. Salesforce notes state the client put the master e-stop on while the engineer restarted OptiSweep and paused/unpaused the tippers. Teams later echoed that the system was back up after restarting OptiSweep and pausing/unpausing the tippers.

title: AGV disconnect history and induction trouble discussed in later support thread
content: The later Teams thread references robot 1542151 as not seen since 9/27, notes that about 24 AGVs were off to the side, asks whether unit 151 was one of the frequent disconnect robots, and mentions a new AGV induction problem with purple lights.
